personality: 7 Dark-First Utility fused with 6 Glass / Luxe premium surfaces
personality_one_liner: A premium, high-contrast, cinematic dark-mode experience that feels like a luxury fighting club—athletic, disciplined, and razor-sharp.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room for cinematic feel, but tight grouping for related class metrics. Use padding to create depth, avoiding cramming content to the edges.

do's: Use glass for the bottom tab bar when content scrolls behind it
Use glass overlays on hero cards paired with background photography
Use glass for sticky header backgrounds during scroll
Apply translucent tinted base (≥75% of surface color) underneath text on glass to ensure readability
dont's: Never apply glass to standard form fields or basic list rows
Do not over-blur; text must remain stark and highly legible
Never blur body content or use glass on dense data/stats cards

screens.name: Login/Registration
screens.purpose: A cinematic gateway into the club, communicating discipline and premium quality instantly.

states.error: Bottom-anchored error toast with red accent and clear message

screens.layout: Full-bleed background image of the gym/ring with a heavy top-to-bottom dark gradient scrim (≥80% opacity at bottom). Logo (Text wordmark in Barlow Condensed if PNG unavailable) anchored top-center. Registration/Login toggle and form inputs anchored in the bottom third over the darkest part of the scrim. Gold (brandPrimary) full-width sticky CTA button at the bottom above safe area.

screens.name: Member Home / Dashboard
screens.purpose: A commanding overview of the member's upcoming training schedule and club news.

states.loading: Dark skeleton loaders for cards and text blocks
states.empty: Illustration of gloves or mat + 'No upcoming classes. Book your next session.'
states.error: Error message with a retry button centered vertically

screens.layout: Sticky header with user's name and avatar. Hero 'Next Class' card (glassmorphism overlay over an atmospheric gym image) showing time, coach, and class type. Below hero: horizontally scrolling 'Announcements' cards (solid surfaceSecondary with border). Below announcements: vertical 'This Week's Schedule' list showing day and class availability.

screens.name: Timetable & Booking
screens.purpose: A high-performance schedule for booking classes with immediate visibility into capacity.

states.empty: Empty state illustration + 'No classes scheduled for this date.'

screens.layout: Date-picker horizontal tape at the top (sticky). Vertical list of class cards. Each class card uses surfaceSecondary. Left side: Time. Right side: Class Name (Barlow Condensed), Coach avatar, and a segmented visual capacity bar (e.g., 18/20 spots filled). Primary CTA 'Book' or 'Join Waitlist' within the card. Warm accents highlight classes filling up.

screens.name: Admin Dashboard
screens.purpose: An operational command center for gym owners to track revenue, members, and daily classes.
screens.layout: Grid of 4 square stat cards at the top (Active Members, Revenue, Pending Approvals, Expiring). Large oversized typography (Barlow Condensed) for numbers. Below: 'Today's Classes' list, tap to open attendance sheet. Bottom: Quick actions row (Add Announcement, Add Member).

screens.name: Coach Check-in / Class Roster
screens.purpose: A fast, high-contrast operational screen for coaches to mark attendance during class.

states.empty: 'No members booked for this class yet.'

screens.layout: Header shows class name and time. Search bar to quickly find member. Vertical list of booked members with their avatar/initials. Right side of each row: High-contrast toggle or 'Check In' button (surfaceTertiary to brandPrimary on active). Focus is purely on speed and glanceability—no decorative images.

hero_bg.alt: Atmospheric MMA fight night and gym environment

class_boxing.alt: A boxer working pads in a modern gym

training_generic.alt: Woman punching a heavy bag in a dark gym

instructions_to_main_agent: Apply a premium dark MMA aesthetic (Dark-First Utility + Luxe). Do not use blues or purples.
Utilize Barlow Condensed for all display titles and numbers (Admin stats, class times, capacities) to give an athletic, structural feel.
Use Manrope (weight 400 or 500) for readable body text, labels, and forms. Never use weights above 500.
Ensure every background image is paired with a top-to-bottom dark gradient scrim (≥80% surfaceInverse opacity at the bottom) before rendering text on it.
Build the capacity indicator on class cards visually, e.g., a progress bar or segmented blocks using brandPrimary for filled spots.
When a screen lists entities (coaches or classes), prioritize the provided high-quality Wix PNG/JPG images over initials.
Implement safe areas meticulously—header content should never hide under notches, and bottom buttons must clear the home indicator.
Use glassmorphism (expo-blur or expo-glass-effect) for sticky headers, the bottom tab bar, and overlays on hero image cards, ensuring a dark base tint underneath for text legibility.
